proven.claim: In dry‑run mode the function only logs; it must NOT modify any counters.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P2
; LINE: 147
; In dry‑run mode the function only logs; it must NOT modify any counters.
; Prove: if dryRun = true then filed, skipped and errors stay 0.
(declare-const dryRun Bool)
(declare-const filed_before Int)
(declare-const skipped_before Int)
(declare-const errors_before Int)

; pre‑condition: counters start at zero
(assert (= filed_before 0))
(assert (= skipped_before 0))
(assert (= errors_before 0))

; transition: the dry‑run branch only prints, no counter update
; (no assignment to filed, skipped, errors)

; negated property: at least one counter changes
(assert (or (distinct filed_before 0) (distinct skipped_before 0) (distinct errors_before 0)))

(check-sat)

proven.claim: All five dry‑run logs are executed only when `dryRun` is true.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P2
; LINE: 147‑152 (dry‑run logs)
; All five dry‑run logs are executed only when `dryRun` is true.
; Prove that in that case the function returns without touching the counters.
(declare-const dryRun Bool)
(declare-const filed_before Int)
(declare-const skipped_before Int)
(declare-const errors_before Int)

; assume dry‑run mode
(assert (= dryRun true))

; counters before entering the dry‑run block
(assert (= filed_before 0))
(assert (= skipped_before 0))
(assert (= errors_before 0))

; transition: no assignments to any counter inside the dry‑run block
; (nothing to assert)

; negated property: at least one counter differs after the block
(assert (or (distinct filed_before 0) (distinct skipped_before 0) (distinct errors_before 0)))

(check-sat)
